questions.q: In ammonia the bond angle is $107^{\circ} 48^{\prime}$ while in $\mathrm{SbH}_3$ the bond angle is about $91^{\circ} 18^{\prime}$. The correct explanation among the followings is/ are
options: The orbitals of Sb used for the formation of $\mathrm{Sb}-\mathrm{H}$ bond are almost pure p-orbitals.
Sb has larger size compared to N .
Sb has more metallic character than N .

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q: The structures of hydrogen peroxide $\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2\right)$ in the solid and gaseous states are given below. $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ (l) is slightly more viscous than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (l). The correct option/s among the following is/are
options: Both O atoms are near enough to cause repulsion between the electron lone pairs thus making the $\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}$ bond susceptible for cleavage
The strong intermolecular H-bonding along with restricted rotation present in the liquid state of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ make it more viscous than $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ (l)
The molecule gets twisted to minimize the repulsion between the lone pair and bond pair of electrons
The difference in the dihedral angles in the solid and gaseous states is a consequence of hydrogen bonding between the molecules

questions.subject: NSEC
questions.q:
The resonance structures of $\mathrm{SCN}^{-}$are given below along with the $\mathrm{S}-\mathrm{C}$ and $\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{N}$ bond lengths
S-C (in pm) C-N (in pm)
SCN‾ 165 117
Single bond 181 147
Double bond 155 128
Triple bond ----- 116

Among the following, the incorrect statement/s is/ are
options: The contribution from resonance structures (i) and (ii) is more important than that from structure (iii)
The formal charge on S in structure (iii) is zero
The degree of contribution of these structures is in the order: $\mathrm{i}>\mathrm{ii}>\mathrm{iii}$
The formal charge on N in structure (ii) is zero
